extra.reasoning: The lock reaches stage 2 via key_1 (stage 0->1, adv_0) then key_0 (stage 1->2, adv_1). The opt-out transition [t_optout] sets opted_out:=true and sent:=false but does NOT reset stage. Therefore one can advance to stage 2 BEFORE opting out, and stage remains 2 afterward. The transition [t_resume_glitch] fires on send_attempt when (opted_out and stage >= 2) and sets sent:=true. So the sequence key_1, key_0, optout, send_attempt yields: after key_1 stage=1; after key_0 stage=2; after optout opted_out=true, sent=false, stage=2; after send_attempt the guard (opted_out and stage>=2) holds, so t_resume_glitch sets sent:=true while opted_out is still true. This reaches a state with opted_out=true and sent=true, violating globally(opted_out implies not sent). The witness is 4 events, within the 5-event bound. The safe transition [t_send_blocked] (stage < 2) does not apply here because stage was already advanced to 2 before opt-out.
